title: Giant Underground Cave Found on Venus—Bigger Than Any on Earth!
summary:
Did you know Venus has a giant underground cave? And it's bigger than any cave like it on Earth!

Scientists found this cave by looking at old radar images taken by a NASA spacecraft called Magellan way back in the 1990s. The images showed a collapsed "skylight"—a hole where the roof of a lava tube had caved in. A lava tube is like an underground tunnel that forms when hot, melted rock (lava) flows and then drains away, leaving an empty space.

This lava tube is near a huge volcano called Nyx Mons (named after a Greek night goddess). The skylight is about 490 feet deep, and the tube itself is at least 1,230 feet deep. But here's the amazing part: scientists think the tube could be up to 1 kilometer wide—that's about 0.6 miles! That's bigger than any lava tube found on Earth or even Mars.

"It is remarkable that we are still extracting new insights from Magellan data," said planetary scientist Anna Gülcher.

Why does this matter? Well, lava tubes on the moon might one day be used as shelters for astronauts. But visiting this one on Venus would be impossible—the pressure there is 93 times greater than on Earth, and it's hot enough to melt electronics!

Two new missions—NASA's VERITAS and Europe's EnVision—will launch soon to study Venus with even better radar. Who knows what other secrets they'll uncover?
why_it_matters: This discovery shows that even places we think we know well, like Venus, can hide giant caves, reminding you to always stay curious about the world.

keywords.explanation: A planet near Earth with thick clouds and a very hot surface.
A big underground hole or tunnel.
An underground tunnel formed when lava flows and then drains away.
A huge volcano on Venus named after a Greek night goddess.
A NASA spacecraft that took radar pictures of Venus in the 1990s.
A future NASA mission to study Venus with better radar.

background_read: Venus is sometimes called Earth's twin because it is almost the same size, but its surface is extremely hot and covered with volcanoes.
Lava tubes are also found on Earth, like the ones in Hawaii, but they are much smaller than this one on Venus.
Scientists use radar to see through Venus's thick clouds because visible light cannot get through.
Article_Structure: WHO: Planetary scientists studying radar images from the 1990s
WHAT: They discovered a huge lava tube, bigger than any on Earth
WHERE: Near the volcano Nyx Mons on Venus
WHY: The finding comes from old Magellan data and shows Venus still holds surprises

perspectives.description: They love finding new things in old data, and this cave might help them understand how lava tubes form on different planets.
Lava tubes could be used as shelters on the Moon or Mars, so learning about them on Venus helps plan future missions.
They may wish astronauts could visit the cave, but Venus is too dangerous; still, it's cool to know it's there!
